description_long: This is a classic stovetop moka pot, commonly used for brewing espresso-style coffee. The body is octagonal and made of matte-finished metal, likely aluminum or stainless steel, with a distinct separation between the upper and lower chambers. A black, side-mounted handle made of heat-resistant plastic is attached to the upper chamber, and a matching black knob sits atop the flat, removable lid. The spout is short and angular, designed for easy pouring, and a small safety valve is visible on the lower chamber. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, with no visible decorative patterns, making it suitable for both domestic and urban outdoor settings.
description: A matte black, octagonal stovetop moka pot with a side handle, flat lid with knob, and a short pouring spout. The design is plain and functional, with a robust metal body and no decorative patterns.
description_view_0: Front view showing the spout, side-mounted handle, and the flat lid with a central knob.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the plain octagonal body, the absence of the handle, and the visible safety valve on the lower chamber.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the handle and spout both visible, emphasizing the functional design.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the handle and the hinge mechanism for the lid, with no spout visible.
